enrichment.summary: Kyle vlogs about his daily life in Boulder, Colorado, covering errands related to getting a motorbike registered and licensed, purchasing marijuana and hash oil from a dispensary called The Farm, and reading aloud from a Colorado legal marijuana consumer guide while adding his own editorial commentary. He also mentions banking overdraft issues with CenturyLink and observations about van-dwellers in a parking lot.
enrichment.speaker: mixed

stances.position: believes he is personally safer driving or riding while high than sober, as it calms his intensity
thinks the official guidance is 'the biggest bunch of bullshit' and overly cautious
finds hash oil significantly cheaper per day despite higher upfront cost; bud gives a better high
strongly warns against crossing into Oklahoma as they have aggressive enforcement and he views it as a war on Colorado
believes marijuana use is a personal responsibility issue and bad outcomes are the individual's fault, not grounds for broad restrictions
doesn't think he can meaningfully distinguish between strains but is willing to try hash vs. bud comparisons

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Today is the tenth day of the V-Log for Boulder.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle was busy all day yesterday getting butter-cycle stuff.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle is not working today because it's too cold.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle received his insurance email and plans to register his motorbike today.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle plans to study for and possibly take a test later today or tomorrow.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle bought a helmet yesterday and wants to get jacking gloves from Craigslist.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle prefers having his own helmet to avoid sharing others' juices.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle bought a medium-sized helmet that feels tight but was told it needs to be snug.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle was tired last night and slept from 10 PM to 9:30 AM.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle switched his bank accounts to avoid overdraft fees caused by CenturyLink.

claims.subject: CenturyLink
claims.claim: CenturyLink keeps dropping calls when trying to resolve overdraft issues.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle plans to call CenturyLink today to ensure he gets the discount he's owed.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle hasn't been to the gym in six months but still pays for it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle slept at the farm and bought bud there for the first time.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The farm is closer to Kyle's house than the previous place he bought bud.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The parking lot near the farm has people living in vans and campers.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle describes the parking lot near the farm as the 'sooner of van living in America.'

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle bought a product called 'Kim dog' for $12, which he considers high-quality.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle bought Rocky Mountain blueberry hash oil hybrid for $75.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle plans to smoke all the bud he bought today, costing him $15 a day.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle finds hash oil cheaper than bud but prefers the high from bud.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle doesn't feel qualified to review different kinds of weed because he can't tell the difference between strains.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle hasn't been sober since marijuana was legalized in Colorado.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle plans to try reviewing hash versus bud.

claims.subject: Colorado
claims.claim: Colorado was the first state to legalize marijuana.

claims.subject: Colorado
claims.claim: Edible marijuana products can take up to two hours to take effect.

claims.subject: Colorado
claims.claim: Marijuana concentrates are stronger and have a quicker effect than regular marijuana.

claims.subject: Colorado
claims.claim: It is illegal to consume marijuana openly in public places in Colorado.

claims.subject: Colorado
claims.claim: Driving under the influence of marijuana is illegal in Colorado and carries serious penalties.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle believes he is safer driving a motor cross bike when high than when sober.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle advises against traveling to Oklahoma if you've smoked weed in the last 30 days due to strict laws.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle plans to stay in Colorado until recreational marijuana is legalized nationwide.
